title: Featured Stories | U.S. Department of the Treasury
summary: This is a Treasury Department listing page aggregating 10 recent news items spanning December 2024 to February 2026, including topics such as whistleblower programs, Iran sanctions, personnel changes, consumer protection research, and housing policy. The page itself contains no substantive macro catalyst analysis or market structure content; it is merely an index of story links.
key_points: Listing page contains no single coherent source—it's an aggregation of 10 separate Treasury news items
Most recent item: Treasury accepting whistleblower tips on fraud, money laundering, and sanctions violations (December 23, 2025)
Iran sanctions item targeting oil network generating 'hundreds of millions of dollars' for Iran's military (January 28, 2025)
Scott Bessent sworn in as 79th Secretary of the Treasury (December 23, 2024)
Research items include purchasing power analysis, well-being comparisons, and housing affordability studies
No economic data releases, policy rate decisions, or market operations present
why_it_matters: This listing page provides no actionable macro catalyst or market structure information. Individual linked stories (particularly the Iran sanctions item) may warrant separate archival, but this index page is not itself a research source.

issues_found: Source is a listing_page per source metadata - violates CORE RULE 5 which mandates rejection of listing pages
Source contains no single coherent publication; items span November 2024 to February 2026
No substantive macro catalyst or market structure content present - page is purely an index of story links
Source attribution is to an aggregate page rather than a specific article
Cannot extract actionable market-moving information from this source type

notes: This source fails the relevance threshold for market structure or macro catalysts. The page is a navigation hub containing links to disparate Treasury announcements, research reports, and administrative updates. No single item on this page constitutes a macro catalyst in the traditional sense (rate decisions, economic data releases, or policy statements with market-moving potential). The Iran sanctions item and Scott Bessent's appointment are the closest candidates but require access to full articles to assess market relevance.
